flashcards.front: What is a matching in graph theory?
flashcards.back: A set of edges without common vertices (pairwise non-adjacent edges)
A set of vertices such that every edge has at least one endpoint in the set
In bipartite graphs, the size of maximum matching equals the size of minimum vertex cover
For a bipartite graph to have a matching covering all vertices in U, for every S ⊆ U, |N(S)| ≥ |S|

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of Hopcroft-Karp algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(√n × m) for finding maximum matching in bipartite graphs
